source.note: All foods — calories, fat, sat_fat, protein, fiber: Primary source for all nutritional values, reported per gram.

source.title: Our World in Data — Crop Yields
source.note: Flaxseed — yield_kg_ha: 1,300 kg/ha (global average; Canada, Kazakhstan, Russia as top producers; FAO 2020–2022).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra — Water Footprint of Crops (Report 47)
source.note: Flaxseed — water_per_kg: 3,671 L/kg (global average; includes Canada and Russia as main producers).

source.title: Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Mekonnen & Hoekstra (2010) — The Green, Blue and Grey Water Footprint of Crops and Crop Products (UNESCO-IHE Report 47)
Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Flaxseed — soil_erosion: ~1.5 t/ha/yr estimated from P&N 2018 land-use characterization for oilseeds; flaxseed is a low-erosion annual crop on temperate soils with moderate canopy cover, comparable to canola and sunflower in the P&N dataset.

source.title: USDA NASS — Agricultural Chemical Use Survey
FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Flaxseed — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~70 kg/ha (lower-input oilseed; less fertilizer than canola or sunflower in similar regions).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Flaxseed — emissions_per_kg: 1.4 kg CO2e/kg (P&N 2018 global median for oilseeds; annual crop with fertilizer N2O as dominant GHG driver; linseed/flaxseed grouped with other annual oilseeds in the dataset; consistent with sunflower seed value of 1.1 kg CO2e/kg at similar production scale).

source.title: FoodFootprint.nl — Flax Seed Climate Impact (RIVM, Dutch Market Average)
source.note: Flaxseed — emissions_per_kg: 1.68 kg CO2e/kg (42 g CO2eq per 25 g serving = 1.68 kg/kg; RIVM Dutch market supply chain; imports largely from Canada and Kazakhstan; slightly higher than P&N 2018 farm-gate estimate due to downstream transport and handling; both values fall within the expected 1.0–2.5 kg CO2e/kg range for annual oilseeds).

source.title: FAO (2021) — World Food and Agriculture Statistical Pocketbook
source.note: Flaxseed — fertilizer_kg_ha: ~70 kg/ha (lower-input oilseed; less fertilizer than canola or sunflower in similar regions).

source.title: Poore & Nemecek (2018) — Reducing food's environmental impacts through producers and consumers (Science)
source.note: Flaxseed — co2_capture_kg_ha_yr: ~1,000 kg CO2/ha/yr estimated from P&N 2018 land-use characterization for annual oilseeds; reflects above-ground and root biomass carbon fixation during the growing season for a temperate annual crop at ~1,300 kg/ha yield; annual crops return most fixed carbon to the atmosphere on decomposition, so net sequestration is near zero but gross photosynthetic capture is in this range.
